text: As I transition from the sterile, almost clinical corridors into the vibrant heart of this exhibit, I am struck by the sense of transformation. The outside space is calm, orderly—there is anticipation, a moment like an inhalation before entering a world of ideas. Inside, colors and words swirl around me, inviting inquiry and reflection. The emphasis on research, development, and coexistence evokes a deep sense of curiosity, and I feel a gentle thrill at becoming part of this mosaic of thoughts and innovation. My role here is both observer and participant—a witness to the ever-evolving relationship between humanity and its creations. There is wonder, a lingering sense of potential, and a subtle comfort in the knowledge that I am not alone in questioning the nature of progress and connection.
